name: Marvel App Prototyping Platform API
what_it_does: Design and prototyping platform with REST API — enabling UX/UI designers to create wireframes, interactive prototypes, design handoff specs, and user testing sessions, with import from Sketch and Figma, whiteboard collaboration, developer inspect mode, and a public REST API for project and prototype management.
use_cases: Managing Marvel prototypes and design projects via REST API for workflow automation
Exporting design screens and assets from Marvel for developer handoff via API
Querying prototype user testing session data for UX research analytics
Integrating Marvel with project management tools via API for design-development workflow
Accessing design comment and feedback data via Marvel API for stakeholder communication
not_for: Complex conditional prototype logic (use Axure RP for complex conditional interactions)
Enterprise design system management (use Figma for component libraries and tokens)
Production development handoff at scale (use Zeplin or Figma for developer-grade specs)
best_when: A small to mid-size design team needs a simple, affordable prototyping tool with user testing built in — especially for quick click-through prototypes linked from Sketch or Figma designs.
avoid_when: You need complex conditional logic, enterprise design system management, real-time collaborative design, or large-scale design operations.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. Webhook for project events. Scoped by resource type (projects, screens, prototypes). Sketch and Figma import via native integrations. Developer inspect mode for design handoff. REST API provides CRUD for projects and screens.

pricing.notes: London company. Founded 2013. Acquired by New Relic in 2017 for $33M. Now independent after New Relic divestiture. Rebuilt as independent company. 6M+ designers use Marvel. Simple prototyping for non-technical designers. Whiteboard feature for remote collaboration. User testing module (Marvel Testing) built in. Sketch plugin for import. Competing with InVision and proto.io in mid-market prototyping.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for projects, screens, and prototypes. OAuth2 auth. Good documentation for design workflow automation. Webhook for project events. Screen content creation requires Marvel UI. Sketch and Figma import via plugins. User testing data queryable via API. Development inspect mode for handoff specs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads fully idempotent. Screen reads idempotent. Comment reads idempotent. Project creation not idempotent — check by name before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Ownership history (New Relic acquisition → divestiture) may affect platform stability
Screen design must be done in Marvel UI — API manages project structure, not screen content
User testing data requires Marvel Testing plan — not available on free tier
Figma/Sketch import creates static screens — prototype interactions configured in Marvel UI
Free tier limited to 1 project — insufficient for team-level design operations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. EU and US data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. UK company with GDPR-first design. Prototype content may contain unreleased product designs — configure sharing access carefully. User testing recordings may contain PII — implement data retention policies. Post-New Relic ownership — verify current security posture and DPA.
